manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: merit, parallelism, expedience, impassion, superiority, imperativeness, interpretation, indecision, expertise.
Your opponent's team words are: evasiveness, ineptitude, glorification, ethic, belief, unfaithfulness, cautiousness, deinstitutionalization.
Distractor words are: bias, audacity, limitlessness, abolitionism, antihuman, despondency, heyday.
The kill words are: implication.

message.content:
interpretationCLUE: evaluation
TARGETS: merit, expertise, interpretation
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words, without using any words from any list. We must list the target words we intend. Must ensure clue not part of any words in lists (including opponent, distractor, kill). Also cannot contain parts of them.

Our list: merit, parallelism, expedience, impassion, superiority, imperativeness, interpretation, indecisiveness? Actually indecision (spelled indecision). expertise.

We can aim for clue that links multiple. Possibly "skill" relates to expertise, merit, superiority maybe? But "skill" might be related. However "skill" part of "expertise" essentially synonyms. Is "skill" allowed? Not in any list. Check not a substring of any listed words. "skill" not in any.

What words would skill relate to? Expertise, merit, superiority? Could be "competence". "competence" not in list. Could relate to expertise, merit, superiority, imperativeness? maybe not.

Maybe "priority" relates to imperativeness, expedience, superiority? Could also relate to merit? Not sure.

Maybe "choice" relates to indecision, interpretation? Not quite.

We could clue "decision" but that's similar to indecision (opponent?). "decision" includes "deci" part but not in list. But our target includes indecision (which is opposite). Might be risky.

We could choose "analysis" relates to interpretation, expertise. That's fine.

But we want to cover many of our words. Could pick "efficiency" relates to expedience, imperativeness? But not others.

Maybe "drive" relates to impassion, superiority, imperativeness. But ambiguous.

Probably safest to give clue that relates to 2-3 words. Let's pick "analysis". It relates to interpretation, expertise, maybe merit (analysis of merit). Could be fine. No conflict.

But we need ensure clue is not part of any word in lists. "analysis" not part of any listed word. Good.

Target list: interpretation, expertise. Maybe also merit? hmm.

Better clue "interpretation" obviously can't be clue because target. Must be different.

Maybe "evaluation" relates to merit, expertise, interpretation. Not in list.

Clue "evaluation". Not part of any list.

Targets: merit, expertise, interpretation. That's three.

Check "evaluation" contains "eval" not in any word list. okay.

Thus output:

CLUE: evaluation
TARGETS: merit, expertise, interpretation

Make sure format exactly:

CLUE: evaluation
TARGETS: merit, expertise,
